articles.title: Strike a Pose
articles.author: Sophia Miranda
articles.dek: Lincoln seniors show off their prom fashion in an annual tradition.
articles.body: With prom season upon us, our senior class has thought of a great way to sneak peak what the prom fashions will look like this year. With the prom theme this year being "Upper East Side New York", the prom fashion show is to show off our beautiful and handsome senior class dressed up in their prom attire. With their beautiful dresses and suits we are sure to be in for a high fashion prom. The light and music set-up simulated a real fashion show one might see in the Upper East Side. Coordinated by our senior cabinet committee, Senior Class president Ivy Chieng said, "It gave me a different perspective on how seniors feel about prom and how this show excited the senior class to want to go to prom and it was very heartwarming to hear such positives comments about our show and we as a cabinet committee were very pleased with the positive feedback from everyone." With the show happening every year the senior class is committed to making a great experience for the seniors to excite them for their special night. Senior Noel Mendoza said, "I think the show went very well, and we had a great turn out and we were so happy to have so many people involved." Excitement for prom is through the roof, and it's sure to deliver.

articles.title: Acadeca Takes Over the State
articles.author: The Railsplitter
articles.dek: Lincoln's Academic Decathlon team competes at regionals with eyes on state.
articles.body: On January 24, Lincoln High School's Academic Decathlon team competed in the first portion of regional competition. Academic Decathlon (ACADECA) is an academic competition in which students study ten subjects under one overarching theme. This year's topic is the Roaring 20's, an era of jazz, economic change, and cultural rebellion. Competitors are tested subjectively in math, social science, science, literature, music, art, and economics, and objectively through speech, interview, essay, and the Super Quiz. At our school, ACADECA is run by Magnet Coordinator Ms. Kwan and AP Seminar teacher Ms. Crabtree, who guide students from the moment the official resources are released in May all the way through competition season. The team is divided into groups based on GPA: Honors (3.8+): Ivan Chi, Yanna Kwong, Tiffany Nguyen Scholastic (3.5â3.8): Abby Guerra, Jiali Huang, Tira Cam Varsity (3.0â3.4): Andre Chen, Yawen Liang, Jieya Liu The JV team consists of Sandra Portillo, Xinyue Huang, David Lin, Jachym Taussig. The ACADECA competition took place at Garfield High School, where students gathered in a large room filled with competitors from other schools. Each academic test lasted 30 minutes, with approximately 50 questions per subject. Later, students faced one of the most personal parts of the competition: interviews and speeches, where judges evaluated not just knowledge, but confidence, communication, and character. Abby Guerra, current Scholastic member, shared her thoughts after the competition. "I have full confidence in our team securing some medals," Abby said. "Jiali is the second highest scorer in all of LAUSD from scrimmages alone." When asked about making it to state, Abby didn't hesitate. "Of course. The team has prepared rigorously and with all their might." But for Abby, state isn't just about medals. "State is going to be really fun. Last year, everyone was friends with everyone. It felt like a family. There was love all around. It brought us closer, and we kept hanging out all year. As the team looks ahead to state competitions later this year, the coaches and students alike are excited not just for the chance to win, but for one more opportunity to learn, grow, and experience something special together.

articles.title: Pi Day, A Never-Ending Joy to the Math-Mind
articles.author: Jada Guevara
articles.dek: Lincoln's MST department celebrates Pi Day with a digit-recitation contest.
articles.body: On March 13th, to celebrate Pi Day, MST planned a competition to see who could count the most digits in the infinite number of Pi. The winner, who counted up to 153 digits was Jiayu Li. She is a ninth grader and participated on behalf of Ms. Sara's advisory class. She was proud to be the Pi winner, but felt she could have recited more and continued to reach a higher goal. She said, "I originally remembered 400 digits, but was stopped by the judges due to a misconception of getting a digit wrong." She prepared in many ways and one thing she mentioned was that she prepared by using a song focused on Pi. She chose to do this competition because before high school she was the president of her middle school. She participated in her school competitions and wanted to give it a try at Lincoln. Previously she got to 150 digits and this year she passed her record by 3 digits. She hopes she will do better and go higher next year. After this event, and during lunch, the teachers and ASB set up a "pie in the face" event where many teachers participated and all the students had fun. Pi is the special number when you divide any circle's circumference (distance around a circle) by its diameter (distance across). This number is infinite, but what makes it special is that it doesn't repeat. Pi is an infinite number, up till this day math solvers are trying to find the concluding digit of pi. Pi is crucial in everyday lives. For instance, engineering, architecture, technology, space exploration, and more use Pi. So, if you want to participate next year, just look at a circle and count 3.1415926â¦.

articles.title: Baseball Is Back and the Dodgers Are At the Top of Their Game
articles.author: Aaron Ortiz
articles.dek: The MLB regular season is underway, and the defending champion Dodgers are off to a strong start.
articles.body: After almost five months since the World Series, Major League Baseball is back. The 2026 regular season begins "Opening Night" on Wednesday, March 25 where the New York Yankees traveled to San Francisco to face the Giants at Oracle Park. This is the first year when Netflix streamed the game at 5:00 pm. The game also featured the first use of the Automatic Ball-Strike (ABS) system in a Major League contest. Caballero tapped his helmet to challenge a fourth-inning called strike from Webb, which was confirmed. "I wanted to go for it," Caballero said. "I thought it was a little higher than what it showed, but at least it was close." The following day, on Thursday, March 26, all eyes were on Dodger Stadium as returned to play after winning back-to-back World Series titles. The Dodgers started their season off by playing their National League West Division rivals, the Arizona Diamondbacks, for a three game series at Dodger stadium.. World Series MVP Yoshinobu Yamamoto took the mound for the Dodgers against Diamondbacks' pitcher Zac Gallen. The Dodgers opened the season in strong fashion, completing a series sweep of the Arizona Diamondbacks as they begin their pursuit of a third consecutive World Series title. The start of the season marks a clean slate for all thirty teams, but for the Dodgers this season can be the one that really cements their history as one of the greatest dynasties in baseball if they win it all in October.

articles.title: Track Takes the Field
articles.author: Isabella Brown
articles.dek: The 2026 season is underway with about 60 athletes and high expectations.
articles.body: The 2026 track and field season has officially begun. Coach Rattay is the head coach for sprinters and shot putters, and Coach Subuyuj coaches long distance. The entire track and cield team consists of about 60 athletes. Coach Rattay has been coaching at Lincoln for many years and in different sports as well. He is passionate in what he does, and encourages others to do their best, simultaneously gaining confidence. Coach Rattay has noted that the team has gotten smaller as the season went on. He has lost some of his best sprinters. This does not stop Coach Rattay from inspiring his runners to improve and challenge themselves. Coach Subuyu has consistently motivated his students to strive for higher times and even some that seem impossible. There have been two preseason track meets, and one official track meet. The coaches as well as the athletes say they are most excited to compete against Wilson because they are the rival school, but also Eagle Rock because they bring competition. Another competitive school, Bravo, has a very large and competitive long distance team, and our distance team is excited to compete against them. There are three players being spotlighted for their incredible hard work and training, Layla Velazquez, Keiana Douglas and Diego Salgado. Layla has shown that she is capable of achieving faster times and being a team player, helping out her fellow teammates. Keiana Douglas is only a freshman and most definitely a talented runner. She competes with other varsity athletes in the hurdle races and does extremely well. Diego Salgado is one of the fastest long distance runners, won many of his races, and is recognized by his coaches. It ss highly suggested that everyone should go to a track meet and support all Lincoln athletes.

articles.title: Spring 2026: What's Trending in LA
articles.author: Audrie Ozuna
articles.dek: From K-town cafes to the Getty, here's where Lincoln students should head this spring.
articles.body: Hey Tigers! Spring is here which means the weather is getting a little warmer and closer to summer and for some, this also means you are a little closer to graduation! But not just yet, it's only spring! So as the flowers bloom and the cool crisp air grazes our faces and sits with our soul in this refreshing season, here are some currently trending things you can do! I would first start off with a trip to a nice cute cafe to grab a bite, some matcha or even a coffee. I recommend a few places and those are Community Goods, Olive and James Cafe Tea in West Hollywood; Memory Look, DAMO, Rok, and Stagger in Koreatown; and Archives Of Us in Chinatown. All of these are great spots to try if you've been craving an iced matcha latte or latte! After a trip to get a drink, I think it'd be nice to find something to do! I recommend visiting The Academy Museum of Motion Picture. It is free entry if you're a student, just don't forget to bring your student ID. This museum is a great place to visit if you have an interest in movies, film or behind the scenes. If that isn't your type of vibe then you should visit either the Getty museum or Getty Villa these places come with nice views! One overlooking the coast and the other overlooking all the westside of Los Angeles, and they're both nice views and you only have to pay for parking for both, entrance is free! All of these things are just some of my recommendations as someone who enjoys exploring and trying new things, I recommend you tigers try them as well!

articles.title: Are Video Games Losing Their Allure?
articles.author: Rebecca Medrano
articles.dek: Fortnite's v-buck inflation and Roblox's controversial updates are driving players away.
articles.body: It's no secret that Epic games is losing players due to their many unnecessary collaborations with Fortnite in hopes of gaining more funds off their player base. Fortnite isn't gaining users as much as they usually do and with their recent choice to hike up prices for v-bucks, an in game currency, what was once originally 1,000 v-bucks has lowered to 800 v-bucks all for the same price. People are in outrage because previously players with the Crew Pass once earned 1,000 v-bucks every month but the price of v-bucks has dropped to 800. In addition, the seasonal battle passes no longer give people the same amount of rewards. V-bucks inflation is a huge cause of many players quitting the game. Recent updates include collaborations with bands, other video games, musicians, television shows, and much more. You'd think people would be happy with all of these collaborations but no--quite the opposite in fact! Players have been complaining about them. Epic games isn't the only company on a downward spiral. Popular mobile game Roblox is completely destroying their game updates disguised as safety but it is now becoming controversial. Players of Roblox are in disarray with the recent "chat ban" in order to prevent grooming, however with the mandatory face scan to verify age that is so easily broken through. Pople wonder just how seriously Roblox is attempting to act. Some people have used photos of celebrities or paper drawings, even using makeup to add facial hair to appear older, and even using filters to break the face scanner. Roblox has also made claims to remove items such as classic faces, classic clothes, r15 mode, and even completely remove stiff bodies for the sake of the more "dynamic" clothes and avatars, and people are outraged by all the changes and have protested to no avail. Roblox only bent to the whims of their community after facing a huge drop in profit. Other gaming companies are on the rise. CAPCOM recently skyrocketed in popularity and sales due to their most recently published game Resident Evil 9 Requiem. The Resident Evil franchise is well known for their characters but beyond that the game features in depth lore and world building that most other games don't do, or put little to no effort into due to something called "second-screening" which more or less means that people would rather have fast pace consumption of social media while having something else in the background for either noise or "aesthetic" --they'd rather have their phone time not be interrupted by whatever they have in the background. All in all, video games aren't really losing their appeal. Players are just getting tired of the unfair changes and updates. Games that focus on fun, creativity, and listening to their community are still successful. In the end, players will stick with games that treat them fairly.

articles.title: Fake News: Can You Tell the Difference?
articles.author: Jennifer Month-Hernandez
articles.dek: A Lincoln survey reveals just how easily misinformation spreads among students.
articles.body: Today it is hard to discern real information from fake news. Fake news can lead to chaos and confusion. The ability to distinguish real information from fake news has become crucial. The rapid spread of news on social media today makes it essential to distinguish real events from false ones in order to make informed decisions and maintain a healthy transparent society. Case in point is a bizarre story of a man named Ricky Hollowell who was allegedly kidnapped for 3 days by a dolphin named "Gerold " off the coast of Fort Myers beach near Sanibel Causeway Florida. According to the tale, Mr Hollowell was taken by "Gerold", a dolphin, for three days to build an underground city. When Mr Hollowells was attended by urgent care he was found dehydrated but with no severe injuries. Although this is an interesting story, this is also entirely fabricated information by a fakebook account page called "The dark humor report". This story later became viral and spread throughout other social media like X, Instagram, and tiktok with other individuals making jokes referencing these alleged events. Another event that viralized was the alleged claim about the alleged death of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's, this case also through social media and was circulated world wide, but the Israeli government officially dismissed it as fake news. About 86% of global online citizens are exposed to false news online. This is because it is engineered to look real and capture users' attraction. At Lincoln through a survey conducted by The Railsplitter 44% of 50 students surveyed believed that the story of Ricky Hallowell may have happened. Out of 73 students surveyed 50.7% of the students claim they check to see if the information they see on social media is real or not. This highlights how easily misinformation can spread and the importance of questioning sensational stories online in a world where viral content thrives, critical thinking and fact checking are our best tools to separate truth from fiction.

articles.title: Boys Volleyball Gets the W
articles.author: The Railsplitter
articles.dek: Lincoln bounces back with a win over Franklin after falling to Wilson.
articles.body: Lincoln lost to Wilson back in March and unfortunately they wound up with the same result on Thursday. The Tigers fell just short of the Mules by a score of 3-2. The Tigers have now taken an 'L' in back-to-back matches. Lincoln took a 2-1 lead headed into the fourth set, but sadly they lost the next two. The match finished with set scores of 25-23, 23-25, 18-25, 25-16, 15-13. It was a great game when Lincoln took on Franklin on Thursday. The Tigers walked away with a 3-1 victory over the Panthers. The Tigers haven't had any issues with the Panthers recently, as the game was their sixth consecutive win against them. While Lincoln might've won, it sure wasn't easy: Franklin scored at least 20 points in every set they played. The final score came out to 25-22, 23-25, 25-23, 25-20. Lincoln now has a winning record of 6-4.

articles.title: Tiger Baseball Comes Roaring Back
articles.author: The Railsplitter
articles.dek: Lincoln blows past the Santee Falcons 9-1 behind standout performances from Paul Martinez and Andrew Ayon.
articles.body: Lincoln bounced back after a loss on the road last Friday to Fremont. They blew past the Santee Falcons 9-1 on Thursday. That large 9-1 mark stands out as the most commanding margin for the Tigers yet this season. Paul Martinez and Andrew Ayon were standout players at the plate. Paul went 2-for-4 with three RBI, one stolen base, and one run, while Andrew earned two runs and four stolen bases. Andrew has now snagged at least one in each of the last four games he's played. Another player making a difference was Bryant Guitron, who went 1-for-2 with two RBI. Lincoln now has a record of 3-4. However, on Friday, April 10, Lincoln came up short against Marshall and fell 5-1.

articles.title: World Baseball Classic 2026
articles.author: Ruby Campos
articles.dek: Venezuela defeats Team USA 3-2 in a dramatic final to claim their first-ever WBC title.
articles.body: Venezuela won their first-ever World Baseball Classic (WBC) title in 2026, defeating Team USA 3-2 in a dramatic final in Miami. The World Baseball Classic showcased the best of international baseball and brought together the best 20 national teams from around the world to compete in one tournament. The competitions were held from March 5th to the 17th across Tokyo, San Juan, Houston, and Miami. The WBC featured some of the best players from in the MLB as well as seventeen year olds playing fot the first time. The competition is divided into 4 different stages. The first stage of the tournament is called pool play where the 20 teams are split into 4 pools of 5. The teams that played in Pool A were Puerto Rico, Cuba, Canada, Panama, Colombia and they all played in San Juan. Pool B had teams like USA, Italy, Mexico, Great Britain, Brazil and played in Houston. Pool C had Japan, Netherlands, Australia, Korea, Czechia and played in Japan. Lastly pool D had Dominican Republic, Venezuela, Israel, Nicaragua, Chinese Taipei playing in Miami. Ultimately pool play determines which teams will advance. Each team faces every other team one time in their group and only the top two teams from each pool moves on based on wins, and tie breakers are determined by who has the best run differentia, so it's important to score more runs to advance. The second stage of the WBC was the quarterfinals where the eight teams that advanced played in a single elimination game. With matchups like the Dominican Republic vs. South Korea, the United States vs. Canada, Italy vs. Puerto Rico, and Japan vs. Venezuela. The Dominican Republic and the USA moved on as expected. While Venezuela shocked the world by defeating the defending champions Japan. Italy also kept their surprising winning streak alive by winning their match ups. The third stage of the WBC is the semifinals where the remaining 4 teams played each other to see who will advance to the championship game. Venezuela continued its historic streak by defeating Italy to finally advance to their first WBC final ever. Meanwhile, the USA beat the Dominican Republic in a close match to earn its 3rd straight trip to the championship game In the final stage of the WBC Venezuela stunned the USA with a 3-2 victory at LoanDepot Park in Miami to win its first WBC title ever. Venezuela looked to lead early on and the USA did briefly tie the game late, but Venezuela had a key go ahead rbi in the 9th inning giving them the win. The win marked a historic moment for Venezuela baseball and capped an exciting tournament full of upsets and dramatic moments.

articles.title: What Annoys You?
articles.author: Samantha Ortiz
articles.dek: Lincoln students share their biggest pet peeves â from slow hallway walkers to phone confiscations.
articles.body: A pet peeve refers to an annoying behavior or action that one finds irritating more than others. Some believe that the root of a pet peeve grows out of a violation of social expectations. According to psychology today, there is a "possibility that what started as a small minor annoyance can stem into a major frustration." School can be an expansion ground for pet peeves on account of the fact that people follow different behaviors that others may not be pleased with. A student expressed that their biggest pet peeve in school is when students walk at a slow pace in the hall. They further revealed that this irritated them because such behavior "blocks their path to class" and sometimes it becomes the reason for why they take so long to get to class. Based on a survey conducted, a certain percentage of students agreed with this behavior as well. Students also shared that the "no cellphone policy" has become rather annoying as well. One student in particular found it bothersome how administration "freaks out" about phones being out in class, especially when there are more important issues to detail with on a daily basis. These devices are then taken away from students which others argue now cannot be used in case of emergencies. In the end, pet peeves reveal how small annoyances can reflect deeper social expectations, concerns and frustrations within a school environment. Recognizing these complexities can help create a more empathetic and effective school culture.

articles.title: Meet Our Campus Mascot: The Little Tiger Cat
articles.author: Sophia Miranda
articles.dek: A little orange cat has taken up residence on campus, brightening mornings for students and staff alike.
articles.body: What is orange, furry and has Tiger spirit? Be on the lookout for what people are calling our real life mascotâ¦the little tiger cat. A little orange cat has been found living on campus and taking its morning laps around the school. The staff and students take turns giving the kitty some treats. It is truly a cute site to see to brighten up your day when walking into school. So get to school bright and early to catch a glimpse of our little mascot.

articles.title: LAUSD Bans Spotify On All Chromebooks
articles.author: Samantha Ortiz
articles.dek: While many students see the popular music streaming platform as a way to relax during class, teachers and administration don't feel the same.
articles.body:
The Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) has recently banned the use of Spotify on all school-issued Chromebooks. The ban, which went into effect on Monday, January 25th, has caused concern among students who rely on the streaming platform for both entertainment and studying.

The district claims the ban is in place to prevent distractions and ensure that students are focused on their academic work. "Spotify can be a huge distraction in the classroom," said LAUSD Superintendent Austin Beutner in a press conference. "We want our students to be able to focus on their studies, and we believe that by banning Spotify, we can help them do that."

However, many students disagree with the ban, arguing that Spotify can actually be a helpful tool for studying. "I listen to Spotify while I study all the time," said Sarah Chen, a junior at Lincoln High School. "It helps me focus and block out distractions. I don't see how it's hurting anyone." Chen also pointed out that many students use Spotify to listen to podcasts and audiobooks for school, which are now also banned.

The ban has also affected students who use Spotify for other purposes, such as listening to music during breaks or while commuting to and from school. "I used to listen to Spotify on my way to school every day," said Mark Johnson, a sophomore at Lincoln High School. "Now I have to find something else to do to pass the time."

The district has not yet announced any plans to lift the ban, and it is unclear how long it will remain in effect. In the meantime, students are encouraged to find alternative ways to entertain themselves during breaks and commutes.

articles.title: LAUSD Students Walk out to Protest ICE Crackdowns
articles.author: Miranda Garcia
articles.dek: Hundreds of students from schools across Los Angeles participated in the walkout on February 4th, protesting the Trump administration's immigration policies.
articles.body:
Thousands of students from schools across Los Angeles participated in a walkout on Monday, February 4th, protesting the Trump administration's immigration policies. According to the Los Angeles Times, approximately 1,500 students from 40 schools participated in the demonstration, which began at 10 a.m. and lasted for approximately two hours.

The students, many of whom were carrying signs and chanting slogans, marched from their respective schools to City Hall, where they gathered for a rally. The protest was organized by a coalition of student groups, including Students Deserve Better and the United Students of Los Angeles. The groups are calling for an end to the Trump administration's ICE crackdowns and for a more humane immigration policy.

"We are here today to demand that our government stop deporting our parents and our families," said Maria Rodriguez, a senior at John Marshall High School, who helped organize the walkout. "We are here to tell them that we will not stand idly by while our communities are torn apart."

The students' demands include a halt to all deportations, an end to the separation of families at the border, and a pathway to citizenship for all undocumented immigrants. They also called for an end to the militarization of schools and for an end to the school-to-prison pipeline.

The protest was met with a mixed reaction from the public. Some people expressed their support for the students, while others criticized them for walking out of class. The Los Angeles Unified School District issued a statement acknowledging the protest and stating that it respects students' rights to express themselves. However, the district also emphasized the importance of attending school and completing coursework.

The walkout is part of a larger movement of student-led protests across the country, which have been organized to demand action on a variety of issues, including gun violence, climate change, and immigration. The students say they will continue to protest until their demands are met.

articles.title: Among the Nation's Best: Lincoln is Recognized for Academic Excellence
articles.author: Samantha Smith
articles.dek: The AP and Magnet awards are presented to those schools that have demonstrated excellence in AP coursework and student achievement.
articles.body:
Lincoln High School has been recognized as one of the best high schools in the nation by the College Board, a non-profit organization that provides standardized tests and college-level courses.

The AP and Magnet awards are presented to those schools that have demonstrated excellence in AP coursework and student achievement. Lincoln High School was one of only 50 schools in the nation to receive both awards, and the only school in Los Angeles Unified School District to do so.

The awards are a testament to the hard work and dedication of the students, teachers, and staff at Lincoln High School. The school has a strong AP program, with a wide variety of courses offered, and a high pass rate on the AP exams. The Magnet program at Lincoln High School is also highly regarded, with a focus on STEM education and a strong track record of preparing students for college and careers.

"We are thrilled to receive these awards," said Principal Emily Lee. "They are a reflection of the commitment of our entire school community to providing the best possible education for our students."

The College Board's AP Program provides students with the opportunity to take college-level courses and exams while still in high school. Students who score well on AP exams may be eligible to receive college credit, which can save them time and money on their college education.

The Magnet Program at Lincoln High School is designed to attract students who are interested in a particular field of study, such as science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). The program provides students with a rigorous curriculum and a supportive learning environment, which prepares them for success in college and beyond.

The recognition from the College Board is a significant achievement for Lincoln High School, and it is a testament to the school's commitment to academic excellence. The awards will help to further enhance the school's reputation and attract more students to its AP and Magnet programs.

articles.title: The $400,000 Admission Letter
articles.author: Ryan Lu
articles.dek: What it takes to be accepted to USC, one of the nation's top universities.
articles.body:
On Monday, January 28th, thousands of students across the country received their admission letters from the University of Southern California (USC). The university, which is ranked among the top universities in the nation, sent out over 60,000 admission letters to prospective students. This year, USC received a record number of applications, with over 100,000 students applying for admission.

The admission process at USC is highly selective, with an acceptance rate of only 15%. The university looks for students who have a strong academic record, as well as those who have demonstrated leadership potential and a commitment to community service. In addition to academics, USC also considers extracurricular activities, essays, and letters of recommendation.

The financial aid offered by USC is also a major draw for students. The university offers a variety of scholarships and grants to help students finance their education. In addition, USC has a partnership with the federal government to provide student loans to those who need them.

USC is committed to providing a well-rounded education to its students. In addition to academics, the university offers a wide range of extracurricular activities, including sports, clubs, and organizations. There are also opportunities for students to participate in research, internships, and study abroad programs.

The admission letter from USC is a dream come true for many students. It signifies not only acceptance to a prestigious university but also the opportunity to pursue their academic and personal goals in a supportive and challenging environment. The journey to USC is one that requires hard work, dedication, and a commitment to excellence, but for those who are accepted, the rewards are immeasurable.

articles.title:
Cooking Club Mixes
Learning and Tasty Treats
articles.author: Katherine Jimenez
articles.dek: Lincoln High School's Cooking Club serves as an opportunity for students to learn about food, culture, and healthy eating habits.
articles.body:
The Cooking Club at Lincoln High School is a relatively new club, but it has already made a big impact on the school community. The club, which meets every Friday after school, is open to all students who are interested in learning more about food and cooking. The club's mission is to provide students with the opportunity to learn about different cultures through their cuisine, as well as to promote healthy eating habits.

The club is led by Ms. Susan Cho, a teacher at Lincoln High School who has a passion for cooking and a desire to share her knowledge with students. "I wanted to create a club where students could learn about food in a fun and interactive way," said Ms. Cho. "I also wanted to provide them with the opportunity to learn about different cultures through their cuisine."

The Cooking Club has already had a number of successful events, including a sushi-making workshop, a pizza-making party, and a bake sale. The club's most recent event was a taco-making class, which was held on Friday, February 1st. The students in the club learned how to make a variety of tacos, including beef, chicken, and vegetarian tacos. They also learned how to make a variety of toppings, such as salsa, guacamole, and sour cream.

The Cooking Club is a great way for students to learn about food and cooking in a fun and engaging way. The club also provides students with the opportunity to learn about different cultures and to develop their healthy eating habits. The club is open to all students, and there is no cost to join. The club meets every Friday after school in the school cafeteria.

articles.title: Multiple Pathways to Success After High School
articles.author: Aaron Ortiz
articles.dek: One of the biggest and deciding decisions seniors face is figuring out what to do after high school graduation.
articles.body:
One of the biggest and most important decisions a high school student will have to make is what to do after graduation. There are many different paths that students can take, and it is important to choose the one that is right for them. Some students may choose to go to college, while others may choose to enter the workforce or join the military. There is no right or wrong answer, and it is important to remember that there are many different ways to be successful.

For those who choose to go to college, there are many different options to choose from. There are four-year universities, community colleges, and trade schools. Each type of school offers a different type of education and experience. Four-year universities offer a traditional college experience, with a focus on academics and research. Community colleges offer a more affordable and flexible option, with a focus on vocational training and job placement. Trade schools offer specialized training in a particular field, such as welding or automotive repair.

For those who choose to enter the workforce, there are many different career paths to choose from. Some students may choose to go directly into the workforce after high school, while others may choose to pursue further education or training. There are many different industries that are hiring, and it is important to find a career that is a good fit for your skills and interests.

For those who choose to join the military, there are many different branches to choose from, including the Army, Navy, Air Force, and Marines. Each branch of the military offers a different type of training and experience. The military is a great option for those who are looking for a challenging and rewarding career, as well as those who want to serve their country.

No matter what path a student chooses, it is important to remember that there are many different ways to be successful. The most important thing is to choose a path that is right for you and to work hard to achieve your goals. There is no one-size-fits-all approach to success, and it is important to remember that everyone is different.

articles.title: A Night at the Theatre: Watching SIX at Hollywood Pantages
articles.author: Aracely Rodriguez
articles.dek: On February 21, 2026, SIX (Sports, Independent, eXtreme) took to the Hollywood Pantages Theatre stage.
articles.body:

articles.title:
Estevan's Top Three
Albums to Stream
articles.author: Estevan Lopez
articles.dek: From classic funk to modern hip-hop, there's something for everyone.
articles.body:
Estevan Lopez, a junior at Lincoln High School, shares his top three album recommendations for anyone looking to stream some new music.

1. "Illmatic" by Nas (1994)

Nas's debut album, "Illmatic," is a hip-hop masterpiece that continues to resonate with audiences today. Released in 1994, the album is a raw and unfiltered look at life in the Queensbridge projects of New York City. The production, handled by a dream team of producers including DJ Premier, Large Professor, and Pete Rock, is innovative and influential. Nas's lyrical prowess is on full display, with intricate rhymes and vivid storytelling that paint a picture of his environment and experiences. Tracks like "N.Y. State of Mind," "The World Is Yours," and "One Love" are considered classics and showcase Nas's ability to craft complex narratives with a smooth, confident delivery. Even decades later, "Illmatic" remains a benchmark for lyrical hip-hop and a must-listen for any serious fan of the genre.

2. "Future Nostalgia" by Dua Lipa (2020)

Dua Lipa's sophomore album, "Future Nostalgia," is a vibrant and infectious collection of dance-pop anthems. Released in 2020, the album draws inspiration from 80s synth-pop and disco, creating a sound that is both retro and modern. Lipa's vocals are powerful and confident, and the production is slick and polished. The album features a string of hit singles, including "Don't Start Now," "Physical," and "Levitating," which have dominated radio airwaves and streaming platforms. "Future Nostalgia" is a testament to Lipa's ability to craft catchy, feel-good music that is perfect for dancing and singing along. It's an album that exudes energy and optimism, making it a perfect choice for lifting spirits and filling the room with a positive vibe.

3. "Kind of Blue" by Miles Davis (1959)

Miles Davis's "Kind of Blue" is widely regarded as one of the greatest jazz albums of all time. Released in 1959, the album is a seminal work of modal jazz, characterized by its cool, understated sound and improvisational freedom. Davis, along with his stellar ensemble including John Coltrane and Cannonball Adderley, creates a mood that is both introspective and expansive. The album's five tracks, including "So What" and "Flamenco Sketches," are masterpieces of mood and melody, inviting listeners into a world of sonic exploration. "Kind of Blue" is not just a jazz album; it's a landmark achievement in music that continues to influence musicians and captivate audiences with its timeless beauty and profound emotional depth.

articles.title: AI Art: the End of Creativity or the Start of a New Movement?
articles.author: Qimei Li
articles.dek: The rise of artificial intelligence in art raises profound questions about authorship, originality, and the future of creative expression.
articles.body:
The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) has infiltrated nearly every aspect of our lives, and the art world is no exception. AI-powered art generators, such as Midjourney, DALL-E 2, and Stable Diffusion, have become increasingly sophisticated, capable of producing stunning and complex visual works from simple text prompts. This technological leap has ignited a fervent debate: Is AI art the harbinger of a new creative renaissance, or does it spell the demise of human artistry as we know it?

On one hand, AI art tools democratize creativity. Individuals without traditional artistic skills can now translate their ideas into visual forms, fostering a new wave of digital expression. AI can serve as a powerful assistant, generating concepts, providing inspiration, and accelerating the creative process for human artists. It can help overcome creative blocks and push the boundaries of what is visually possible, potentially leading to entirely new art movements and aesthetics. The ability to generate countless variations on a theme or to explore styles that would be prohibitively time-consuming for a human artist opens up exciting avenues for exploration.

However, the rise of AI art also presents significant challenges. Concerns about copyright and ownership are paramount, as AI models are trained on vast datasets of existing human-created art, raising questions about fair use and intellectual property. The potential for AI to displace human artists and devalue their labor is another pressing issue. If AI can generate art quickly and cheaply, will there still be a market for human-made art? Furthermore, critics argue that AI-generated art lacks the emotional depth, intention, and lived experience that are integral to human creativity. Art has always been a reflection of the human condition, a means of communication born from personal struggles, joys, and perspectives. Can an algorithm truly replicate that?

The debate over AI art is complex and multifaceted. It is unlikely to be a simple case of either complete replacement or seamless integration. Instead, we are likely to see a dynamic evolution where AI becomes another tool in the artist's arsenal, much like the camera or digital software before it. The challenge lies in finding a balanceâharnessing the power of AI to augment human creativity while ensuring that the intrinsic value of human expression remains protected and celebrated. The future of art may not be solely human or solely AI, but a collaborative dance between the two, forging a new path for creativity in the digital age.
articles.credit: Illustration by Eva Bee / Ikon Images

articles.title: AI Art: The End of Creativity or a Start of a New Movement?
articles.author: Qimei Li
articles.dek: The debate continues as AI-generated art gains traction in the creative world.
articles.body:
In recent years, artificial intelligence (AI) has made significant strides in various fields, including art. AI-generated art, created by algorithms and machine learning models, has become increasingly sophisticated, raising questions about the future of creativity and the role of human artists.

The debate surrounding AI art is multifaceted. Some argue that AI art is a threat to human creativity, devaluing the skills and efforts of artists. They believe that art should be a product of human emotion, intention, and experience, which AI cannot replicate. Others view AI art as a new tool for artists, a means to explore novel forms of expression and push the boundaries of art.

A Japanese artist, Hiyayo Miyazaki, has gained attention for his AI-generated art, which he creates using a combination of AI models and digital manipulation. His work, which often features surreal and dreamlike imagery, has been exhibited in galleries worldwide. Miyazaki believes that AI art is not a replacement for human creativity but rather an extension of it.

"AI can be a powerful tool for artists," Miyazaki says. "It allows us to explore new possibilities and create art that would be impossible to achieve through traditional means. The key is to use AI as a collaborator, not as a replacement for our own creative vision."

However, concerns about AI art persist. Some worry that the widespread use of AI-generated art could lead to a homogenization of artistic styles and a decline in the value of human-created art. Others point to the ethical implications of AI art, such as copyright issues and the potential for AI to be used to create deepfakes or spread misinformation.

As AI technology continues to evolve, the debate surrounding AI art is likely to intensify. Whether AI art is seen as a threat or an opportunity, it is clear that it has already begun to reshape the art world and challenge our traditional notions of creativity and authorship.

articles.title: How Much Sugar Is in Your Drink?
articles.author: Ruby Campos
articles.dek: Comparing the sugar content of popular beverages.
articles.body:
Sugar is a common ingredient in many of our favorite drinks, but its content can vary significantly. This chart illustrates the sugar content of several popular beverages, highlighting the differences in sweetness and potential impact on health.

Coca-Cola and Pepsi, two of the most popular sodas, contain a substantial amount of sugar. A 12-ounce serving of Coca-Cola has approximately 39 grams of sugar, while Pepsi contains slightly more at 41 grams. These figures are concerning, especially when considering the recommended daily sugar intake, which for adults is around 25-30 grams per day.

Arizona Iced Tea, often perceived as a healthier alternative, also packs a significant sugar punch. A 23-ounce serving can contain as much as 47 grams of sugar. Energy drinks like Monster and Red Bull also contribute to high sugar consumption, with a 16-ounce Monster containing 54 grams and a 12-ounce Red Bull containing 37 grams.

Even drinks marketed towards children can be surprisingly high in sugar. A 6.75-ounce Capri Sun contain about 15 grams of sugar, and a 10-ounce Kool-Aid Jammers contain 21 grams. For those seeking lower-sugar options, the chart shows that a 12-ounce Mountain Dew has 46 grams of sugar, a 15-ounce Gatorade has 34 grams, and a 16-ounce Starbucks Frappuccino has 65 grams. Even seemingly healthier options like a 14-ounce Starbucks Vanilla Latte can contain 33 grams of sugar.

The American Heart Association recommends that women consume less than 25 grams of sugar per day and men consume less than 36 grams. The high sugar content in many popular beverages makes it challenging to adhere to these guidelines. It is important to be aware of the sugar content in our drinks and make informed choices to maintain a healthy diet.

articles.title: How to Protect ICE
articles.author: Miranda Garcia
articles.dek: A guide to understanding and safeguarding Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
articles.body:
Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has been at the forefront of immigration policy debates in the United States. Understanding the role and function of ICE is crucial for navigating the complexities of immigration law and policy. This article aims to provide a comprehensive overview of ICE, its mission, and the challenges it faces.

ICE was established in 2003 as part of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). Its mission is to promote homeland security and public safety through the enforcement of federal immigration and customs laws. ICE has a broad mandate, encompassing a wide range of activities, including border security, immigration enforcement, and customs enforcement.

One of the most controversial aspects of ICE's work is its role in enforcing immigration laws. ICE agents are responsible for identifying and apprehending undocumented immigrants, as well as detaining and deporting them. These enforcement actions have been criticized by some as being overly aggressive and inhumane. However, proponents of ICE's enforcement policies argue that they are necessary to maintain the integrity of the immigration system and ensure national security.

Another key function of ICE is customs enforcement. ICE agents work to prevent the illegal entry of goods and individuals into the United States. They also play a crucial role in combating the flow of illicit drugs, weapons, and other contraband across the border.

The agency has also been involved in efforts to combat human trafficking and other forms of transnational crime. ICE agents work with international partners to disrupt criminal organizations and bring perpetrators to justice.

Despite its critical role in national security and immigration enforcement, ICE has faced numerous challenges. These include political polarization, resource limitations, and legal battles over its enforcement practices. The agency's work is often subject to intense public scrutiny, and it must constantly adapt to evolving legal and policy landscapes.

It is important to note that ICE's operations are guided by federal laws and regulations. The agency is subject to oversight from Congress and the Government Accountability Office. Additionally, numerous court cases have shaped the way ICE operates, ensuring that its actions are consistent with the Constitution and federal statutes.

articles.title: Lunar New Year: A Celebration of Family, Tradition, and New Beginnings
articles.author: Megan Nim
articles.dek: Embracing the Year of the Fire Horse with joy and optimism.
articles.body:
Lunar New Year, also known as the Spring Festival, is a vibrant and significant holiday celebrated by billions worldwide. It marks the beginning of a new year on the lunisolar calendar and is a time for families to come together, honor traditions, and welcome good fortune.

This year, the celebration ushers in the Year of the Fire Horse, a symbol of energy, passion, and dynamism. It is believed that those born in the Year of the Horse are adventurous, independent, and enthusiastic, possessing a strong sense of justice and a love for freedom.

The holiday is characterized by a multitude of customs and traditions, each carrying its own unique significance. Families gather for lavish reunion dinners, sharing symbolic dishes that represent prosperity, health, and happiness. Red envelopes, filled with money, are exchanged as tokens of good luck and well wishes, particularly for children and unmarried individuals.

Decorations play a pivotal role in the festivities. Homes are adorned with vibrant red lanterns, intricate paper cuttings, and auspicious calligraphy, all intended to ward off evil spirits and attract good fortune. The color red is particularly significant, symbolizing luck, joy, and prosperity.

"The Lunar New Year is a time for renewal and hope," says Lin Dadi, a descendant of immigrants who has celebrated the holiday for decades. "It's a chance to reconnect with our roots, share our culture with the younger generation, and embrace the promise of a new beginning."

articles.title: Mexico Cartel Leader 'El Mencho' Killing Sparks Chaos
articles.author: Miranda Garcia
articles.dek: The alleged death of a notorious drug lord plunges the region into uncertainty.
articles.body:
The news of the alleged death of Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, also known as "El Mencho," the notorious leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), has sent shockwaves across Mexico and the international community. While authorities have yet to confirm his demise, rumors of his killing in a violent clash with rival cartels have led to widespread speculation and heightened concerns about the escalating cartel violence in the region.

El Mencho, considered one of the most powerful and ruthless drug lords in Mexico, has been a key figure in the country's ongoing drug war for years. His cartel, CJNG, is known for its extreme brutality and its vast network of operations, which span across Mexico and extend into the United States. The group has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and other illicit activities, contributing significantly to the pervasive insecurity in many parts of Mexico.

The alleged death of El Mencho has raised fears of a power vacuum within the CJNG, which could lead to intense turf wars and increased violence as factions vie for control. This could further destabilize regions already grappling with cartel-related crime and pose significant challenges to law enforcement agencies attempting to curb the drug trade.

In response to the escalating situation, Mexican authorities have increased security measures in several states, particularly in Jalisco, the cartel's stronghold. However, the clandestine nature of cartel operations and the deep-rooted corruption within some institutions make it difficult to effectively combat these organizations.

The United States, which has a complex relationship with Mexico regarding drug interdiction efforts, is closely monitoring the situation. El Mencho's alleged death could have significant implications for bilateral cooperation in combating transnational crime and drug trafficking.

As the situation unfolds, the focus remains on the potential for increased violence and instability. The alleged demise of El Mencho marks a significant moment in the ongoing struggle against organized crime in Mexico, and its long-term consequences remain to be seen.

articles.title: Oregon Ducks Dominate in 56-22 Peach Bowl Loss to No. 1 Indiana
articles.author: Emily Cable
articles.dek: The Ducks fall short against the undefeated Hoosiers in a disappointing performance.
articles.body:
The Oregon Ducks' dream of an undefeated season came to a crushing halt in the Peach Bowl on January 1st, 2026, as they suffered a demoralizing 56-22 defeat at the hands of the No. 1 ranked Indiana Hoosiers. The highly anticipated matchup, which was expected to be a close contest, turned into a one-sided affair, with the Hoosiers dominating from start to finish.

The Ducks' offense, which had been a powerhouse throughout the season, struggled to find its rhythm against the Hoosiers' formidable defense. Quarterback Bo Nix, who had been instrumental in Oregon's success, was unable to find his footing, throwing two interceptions and fumbling the ball twice. The offensive line also struggled to protect Nix, as he was sacked multiple times throughout the game.

The Hoosiers, on the other hand, put on a clinic, showcasing their explosive offense and suffocating defense. Quarterback Michael Penix Jr. had a field day against the Ducks' secondary, throwing for five touchdowns and over 400 yards. The Hoosiers' running game was equally impressive, as they consistently pounded the ball downfield, chewing up yards and clock.

The Ducks' only glimmer of hope came in the form of their special teams and a few defensive stands. However, these moments were not enough to overcome the Hoosiers' relentless pressure and superior execution. The loss marks a bitter end to what had been a promising season for Oregon, leaving fans and players alike questioning what could have been.

The Ducks' season concludes with a final record of 12-1, their sole loss coming in the most critical game of the year. The Hoosiers, meanwhile, complete their perfect season, solidifying their claim as the best team in college football.

articles.title: Maureen Dowd's 'Tough Sauce' Goes Too Close to The Sun
articles.author: Emily Cable
articles.dek: A critical review of the columnist's latest work.
articles.body:
Maureen Dowd, the Pulitzer Prize-winning columnist for The New York Times, has always been known for her sharp wit and incisive commentary. Her latest book, 'Tough Sauce,' however, falls short of her usual high standards. While Dowd attempts to tackle complex issues such as political polarization, societal divides, and the erosion of civil discourse, the book ultimately feels disjointed and lacking in depth.

Dowd's signature style of blending personal anecdotes with political analysis is present, but it doesn't quite land. The book jumps from one topic to another without a clear narrative thread, leaving the reader feeling lost. Her attempts at humor often fall flat, and her critiques, while sometimes valid, lack the nuanced approach that has defined her previous work.

One of the book's main weaknesses is its lack of a central argument. Dowd touches upon various issues but fails to connect them into a cohesive whole. This makes it difficult for the reader to grasp her overall message or purpose. The book feels more like a collection of disconnected essays rather than a unified work.

Furthermore, Dowd's reliance on sensationalism and hyperbole detracts from the seriousness of the topics she addresses. While she aims to provoke thought, she often resorts to inflammatory language that alienates rather than engages the reader. This approach undermines the credibility of her arguments and diminishes the overall impact of the book.

Despite its flaws, 'Tough Sauce' does offer some moments of insight. Dowd's observations on the current political climate are often accurate, and her ability to capture the zeitgeist is undeniable. However, these moments are few and far between, and they are not enough to salvage the book from its shortcomings.

In conclusion, 'Tough Sauce' is a disappointing addition to Maureen Dowd's body of work. While it may appeal to her loyal readers, it fails to live up to the expectations set by her previous writings. It is a book that tries to do too much and ultimately achieves too little.

articles.title: Kazakhstan Wins First Olympic Gold Skating Medal

articles.body:
In a historic moment for Kazakhstan, figure skater Mellat 'Mali' Sre clinched the country's first-ever Olympic gold medal in the sport at the 2026 Winter Olympics. Sre, a 21-year-old prodigy, delivered a flawless performance in the ladies' singles event, captivating the audience and judges alike with her artistry, technical prowess, and emotional depth.

Sre's journey to the Olympic podium has been nothing short of remarkable. She began skating at the age of four, inspired by her older sister, who was also a figure skater. Sre quickly developed a passion for the sport, dedicating countless hours to training and honing her skills. Her hard work and perseverance paid off when she won the Junior World Championships in 2022, establishing herself as a rising star in the figure skating world.

At the 2026 Winter Olympics, Sre's performance was nothing short of breathtaking. She executed a challenging routine that included multiple triple axels and complex spins, earning her a record-breaking score. Her artistry and musicality shone through, as she conveyed a powerful narrative through her movements, leaving the audience in awe.

"I'm still in shock," Sre said in a post-event interview. "I've dreamed of this moment since I was a little girl. To be able to achieve it, and to bring this medal home to Kazakhstan, is truly an honor. I want to thank my coaches, my family, and everyone who has supported me along the way."

Sre's victory not only marks a significant milestone for Kazakhstan but also serves as an inspiration to aspiring athletes around the world. Her dedication, resilience, and passion for her sport are a testament to the power of dreams and the pursuit of excellence.

articles.title: Brazil's Lucas Pincheira Shines, Wins South America's First Winter Olympic Gold Medal
articles.author: Emily Cable
articles.dek: The 23-year-old becomes a national hero with his groundbreaking achievement.
articles.body:
In a moment that will be etched in Olympic history, Brazilian freestyle skier Lucas Pincheira has won South America's first-ever Winter Olympic gold medal. The 23-year-old sensation claimed the top spot in the men's slopestyle event at the 2026 Winter Olympics, delivering a performance that was both technically brilliant and artistically inspired.

Pincheira, who hails from the coastal city of Rio de Janeiro, has defied the odds and expectations to achieve this historic feat. Growing up in a country where winter sports are virtually non-existent, he learned to ski on artificial slopes and competed in local events before venturing abroad to pursue his passion.

His journey to the Olympic podium has been marked by dedication, sacrifice, and an unwavering belief in his dreams. He honed his skills through relentless training, pushing the boundaries of what was thought possible in freestyle skiing. His unique style, characterized by daring tricks and a seamless flow, has captivated audiences and judges alike.

In the Olympic final, Pincheira delivered a performance for the ages. He executed a series of breathtaking aerial maneuvers, including a complex triple cork 1440, that earned him a near-perfect score. His runs were a testament to his skill, creativity, and courage, leaving his competitors in awe.

"I'm still trying to process it all," Pincheira said, tears welling up in his eyes. "This is a dream come true. I want to dedicate this medal to my family, my friends, and all of Brazil. I hope this inspires more young people in South America to pursue their dreams, no matter how challenging they may seem."

Pincheira's victory not only brings glory to Brazil but also marks a significant moment for winter sports in South America. His achievement is expected to boost the popularity of skiing and snowboarding in the region, inspiring a new generation of athletes to take up the sport.

articles.title: Mathilde Grenaud Defeats Japanese Superstar in Historic Gold Medal
articles.author: Emily Cable
articles.dek: The French biathlete outshines the favorite in a stunning upset.
articles.body:
In a stunning upset that has sent shockwaves through the biathlon world, French biathlete Mathilde Grenaud has claimed the gold medal in the women's 15km individual event at the 2026 Winter Olympics, defeating reigning champion and pre-event favorite, Hana Ito of Japan.

Grenaud, who has been steadily climbing the ranks in recent years, delivered a masterful performance, combining flawless shooting with a strong skiing pace. She missed only one target in the entire race, a remarkable feat considering the challenging conditions and the pressure of the competition. Her clean shooting, coupled with her exceptional speed on the course, allowed her to edge out Ito by a mere 10 seconds.

Ito, who was seeking her third consecutive Olympic gold medal, put up a valiant fight, but two missed targets in the final shooting stage proved to be her undoing. Despite her disappointment, Ito showed great sportsmanship, congratulating Grenaud on her victory.

"I'm absolutely ecstatic," Grenaud said, tears of joy streaming down her face. "I've worked so hard for this moment. To be able to compete against the best in the world and come out on top is incredible. This gold medal is not just for me, but for my country, my coaches, and everyone who believed in me."

Grenaud's victory marks a significant achievement for France, as it is the country's first Olympic gold medal in women's biathlon in over a decade. Her triumph is expected to inspire a new generation of French athletes and further elevate the profile of biathlon in the country.

The silver medal went to Ito, while Norway's Ingrid Landmark Tandrevold took home the bronze. The women's 15km individual event was a true testament to the sport's excitement and unpredictability, leaving fans on the edge of their seats until the very end.

articles.body:
It's that time of the year again! Abraham Lincoln High School began its annual toy drive this past week, collecting donations for children in need. The drive, organized by the students of the school, aims to provide toys and essential items to children who might otherwise go without during the holiday season.

The toy drive, which kicked off on November 27th, will run until December 15th. Students can donate new, unwrapped toys or gently used books at designated drop-off locations throughout the school. The collected items will be distributed to local shelters and organizations that serve underprivileged children in the community.

"We want to make sure that every child has a chance to experience the joy of the holiday season," said Sarah Kim, a senior and one of the event organizers. "We believe that by coming together as a community, we can make a real difference in the lives of these children."

Last year, the toy drive collected over 2,000 toys, benefiting more than 500 children. This year, the organizers are hoping to exceed that number with the help of the entire school community.

In addition to the toy drive, the school is also collecting canned goods and non-perishable food items for families in need. These donations will be distributed to local food banks and soup kitchens, ensuring that no one goes hungry during the holiday season.
